title: Define predicate types and routing module root
purpose: Establishes the predicate type system that forms the declarative tier of the two-tier routing model (ADR-003). These types are the building blocks for content-aware subscriptions: comparison operators, boolean combinators, range checks, and field-existence tests. The evaluation engine (ROUTING-002) and predicate compiler (ROUTING-003) consume these types; this brief defines them without implementing evaluation logic.
task: Create the routing module root and the predicate type definitions. Define the Predicate enum with variants for comparison, boolean combination, range, and field-existence. Define supporting types: ComparisonOp (six relational operators), FieldValue (typed operand values), and FieldPath (dot-notation message field accessor). Establish the missing-field contract: predicates against absent fields evaluate to false without error. This brief defines types only — evaluation logic belongs to ROUTING-002, compilation to ROUTING-003.

requirements.title: Define comparison operator enum and comparison predicate variant
requirements.spec: The predicate module SHALL define a ComparisonOp enum with exactly six variants (Eq, Ne, Gt, Lt, Gte, Lte) and a Predicate::Comparison variant taking a FieldPath, ComparisonOp, and FieldValue. The ComparisonOp enum SHALL NOT include pattern-matching or regex operators.
acceptance: ComparisonOp enum has exactly six variants: Eq, Ne, Gt, Lt, Gte, Lte.
Predicate::Comparison { field: FieldPath, op: ComparisonOp, value: FieldValue } constructs a valid predicate.
ComparisonOp implements Clone, Debug, PartialEq, and Eq.
All six ComparisonOp variants round-trip through Debug formatting.

requirements.title: Define boolean combinator predicates for arbitrary composition
requirements.spec: The Predicate enum SHALL include And, Or, and Not variants for composing predicates into arbitrarily nested expressions. And and Or SHALL accept a Vec<Predicate>. Not SHALL accept a Box<Predicate>. Composition depth SHALL NOT be artificially limited.
acceptance: Predicate::And(vec![p1, p2]) composes two predicates with logical AND.
Predicate::Or(vec![p1, p2]) composes two predicates with logical OR.
Predicate::Not(Box::new(p)) negates a single predicate.
Nested composition Predicate::And(vec![Predicate::Comparison{..}, Predicate::Or(vec![..])]) constructs without error.
Empty And(vec![]) and Or(vec![]) are valid constructions.

requirements.title: Define range-check and field-existence predicate variants
requirements.spec: The Predicate enum SHALL include a Range variant taking a FieldPath and two FieldValue bounds (lower inclusive, upper inclusive) for numeric and string field comparisons. The Predicate enum SHALL include an Exists variant taking a FieldPath for testing field presence. Range SHALL NOT support exclusive bounds in this brief.
acceptance: Predicate::Range { field: FieldPath, lower: FieldValue, upper: FieldValue } constructs a range predicate.
Predicate::Exists { field: FieldPath } constructs a field-existence predicate.
Range with FieldValue::Integer(100) lower and FieldValue::Integer(200) upper compiles without error.
Range with FieldValue::Text("a".into()) lower and FieldValue::Text("z".into()) upper compiles without error.

requirements.title: Define FieldValue enum, FieldPath accessor, and missing-field contract
requirements.spec: The predicate module SHALL define a FieldValue enum with variants for String, Integer (i64), Float (f64), Boolean, and Null. The module SHALL define a FieldPath type wrapping a dot-notation string with a segments accessor. Predicates evaluated against a message missing the referenced field SHALL evaluate to false without returning an error. The FieldValue type SHALL NOT carry binary or structured (nested object) values.
acceptance: FieldValue has exactly five variants: Text(String), Integer(i64), Float(f64), Boolean(bool), Null.
FieldPath::new("user.address.city") constructs a valid path.
FieldPath::segments() on "user.address.city" returns an iterator yielding ["user", "address", "city"].
FieldPath::new("name") with no dots yields a single segment ["name"].
FieldValue implements Clone, Debug, and PartialEq.

requirements.title: Wire routing module root with pub mod and re-exports
requirements.spec: The routing module root SHALL contain only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports per CN3. All public predicate types SHALL be re-exported from the routing module root.
acceptance: routing/mod.rs contains pub mod predicate and no function or trait definitions.
Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, and FieldPath are accessible via use crate::routing::{Predicate, ComparisonOp, FieldValue, FieldPath}.
No logic (functions, trait impls, match arms) exists in mod.rs.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement predicate evaluation logic — evaluation belongs to ROUTING-002.
SHALL NOT implement predicate-to-function compilation — compilation belongs to ROUTING-003.
SHALL NOT define routing table or subscription types — those belong to ROUTING-002.
SHALL NOT define backpressure or capacity types — those belong to ROUTING-005.
SHALL NOT add serialization/deserialization derives unless required for type construction tests.
verification: cargo clippy -p liminal --all-targets -- -D warnings passes clean.
cargo test -p liminal passes with all predicate type construction tests green.
routing/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use — no logic.
predicate.rs is under 500 lines including tests (CN1).
